reasoning: The document exhibits significant content and structural failures that the programmatic signals did not fully capture, despite a shingling recall of 0.913. The LLM fidelity report identifies a major omission of over 30 department names (constituting 60% of a section) and the corruption of a project status table. Given the low structural score (0.5) and the 3x cost penalty for false negatives, this document requires human intervention to restore critical data.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Two significant text omissions were identified. First, in the 'Potential Participants' section (page 8), the output fails to include the approximately 30 department names listed as sub-items under the eight main categories (e.g., VPN Administrator Access). This constitutes a loss of over 60% of the section's content, which is a MAJOR defect. Second, in the 'Introduction' section (page 5), the descriptive sub-bullets defining types of authentication (e.g., 'Something you know...', 'Something you
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1. VPN (Administrator Access) o Department of Information Technology o Department of Public Safety o Department of Revenue o Department of Human and Health Services o Department of Natural and Cultural Resources o Department of Administration o Department of Transportation

concerns.analysis: The table structure for the MFA Project status on page 10 is corrupted. In the output's 'Plan' section, the first table (element 79) is structurally inconsistent: the first four rows contain 2 columns, while the fifth row contains 3 columns. This results in misaligned data and a broken table structure compared to the visual alignment in the source.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Project Status list on page 10 showing '5. Procurement Phase Pending Jan 2016' as a single entry area.
concerns.output_quote: The table with index 79 has rows with differing cell counts (e.g., Row 1 has 2 cells, Row 5 has 3 cells: '5. Procurement Phase', 'Pending', 'Jan 2016').

concerns.analysis: The list structure is significantly degraded. On page 8, under 'Potential Participants', the source contains a complex nested list (numbered items with multiple bullet points under each). The output (element 52) renders this as a flat list of 8 items, completely omitting all nested bullet points and their associated content. Similarly, the sub-bullets under 'Types of authentication include' on page 5 are missing from the list in element 27.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1. VPN (Administrator Access) o Department of Information Technology o Department of Public Safety ... [and so on for 8 numbered items with bullets]
concerns.output_quote: A flat list of 8 items containing only the numbered headings (e.g., 'VPN (Administrator Access)', 'Payment Card Industry Servers'). All indented bullet points are absent from the structure.
concerns.location: Potential Participants section, page 8, element index 52

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: A minor content fidelity issue exists in the 'Legislative Request' section on page 5, where the reference URL was omitted. This results in a broken sentence: 'See for reference.' instead of 'See http://www.ncleg.net/Sessions/2015/Bills/House/PDF/H97v9.pdf for reference.' Additionally, the missing sub-bullets in the Introduction (noted in C5) serve as a second minor content omission that alters the level of detail available to the reader.

A significant government URL present in the source is missing from the output. On page 5, the text 'See http://www.ncleg.net/Sessions/2015/Bills/House/PDF/H97v9.pdf for reference.' is split into two elements (19 and 21), and the link itself (index 20 in the logical sequence) is absent.
concerns.output_quote: The text around the link exists ('See' in element 19 and 'for reference.' in element 21), but the URL and link element are missing.
concerns.location: Legislative Request section, page 5, between element 19 and 21

action_items: Restore the list of 30+ department names under the 'Potential Participants' section
Reconstruct the MFA Project status table on page 10 to ensure column alignment
Fix nested bullet points on pages 5 and 8
